# SKILL: Azure Deployment

## Overview
Production-grade deployment patterns for TypeScript/Next.js applications on **Microsoft Azure** — Static Web Apps, Container Apps, App Service, and Azure DevOps pipelines.

## Option A: Azure Static Web Apps (Best for Next.js/JAMstack)
```yaml
# .github/workflows/azure-static-web-apps.yml
# (Auto-generated by Azure Static Web Apps wizard — then customized)
name: Azure Static Web Apps CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, closed]
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build_and_deploy:
    if: github.event_name == 'push' || (github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.action != 'closed')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with: { submodules: true, lfs: false }

      - name: Build And Deploy
        uses: Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1
        with:
          azure_static_web_apps_api_token: ${{ secrets.AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN }}
          repo_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          action: 'upload'
          app_location: '/'           # Next.js app root
          api_location: 'api'         # Azure Functions (optional)
          output_location: '.next'    # Build output
          app_build_command: 'npm run build'

  close_pull_request:
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.action == 'closed'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1
        with:
          azure_static_web_apps_api_token: ${{ secrets.AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN }}
          action: 'close'
```

```bash
# Azure CLI setup for Static Web Apps
az login
az staticwebapp create \
  --name "my-nextjs-app" \
  --resource-group "rg-production" \
  --source "https://github.com/org/repo" \
  --location "eastus2" \
  --branch "main" \
  --app-location "/" \
  --output-location ".next" \
  --login-with-github
```

## Option B: Azure Container Apps (Docker — Recommended for APIs)
```typescript
// infra/main.bicep — Infrastructure as Code
// Azure Container Apps (serverless containers — scales to 0)
```

```bash
# Deploy via Azure CLI
az group create --name rg-production --location eastus2

# Create Container Apps Environment (shared infra for multiple apps)
az containerapp env create \
  --name env-production \
  --resource-group rg-production \
  --location eastus2

# Deploy container
az containerapp create \
  --name api-app \
  --resource-group rg-production \
  --environment env-production \
  --image myacr.azurecr.io/api:latest \
  --target-port 3000 \
  --ingress external \
  --min-replicas 1 \
  --max-replicas 10 \
  --cpu 0.5 --memory 1.0Gi \
  --secrets "db-url=keyvaultref:https://kv-prod.vault.azure.net/secrets/DATABASE-URL" \
  --env-vars "NODE_ENV=production" "DATABASE_URL=secretref:db-url"
```

## Option C: Azure App Service (Traditional PaaS)
```bash
# Create App Service Plan
az appservice plan create \
  --name plan-production \
  --resource-group rg-production \
  --sku B2 \          # B2 = 2 vCPU, 3.5GB RAM
  --is-linux

# Create Web App (Node.js)
az webapp create \
  --name my-api \
  --resource-group rg-production \
  --plan plan-production \
  --runtime "NODE:22-lts"

# Configure app settings (env vars)
az webapp config appsettings set \
  --name my-api \
  --resource-group rg-production \
  --settings \
    NODE_ENV=production \
    WEBSITE_NODE_DEFAULT_VERSION=22-lts

# Deploy from GitHub
az webapp deployment source config \
  --name my-api \
  --resource-group rg-production \
  --repo-url https://github.com/org/repo \
  --branch main \
  --manual-integration
```

## GitHub Actions → Azure Container Registry + Container Apps
```yaml
# .github/workflows/azure-deploy.yml
name: Deploy to Azure

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  id-token: write   # OIDC — no long-lived secrets
  contents: read

env:
  REGISTRY: myregistry.azurecr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: api-app

jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Azure Login (OIDC — no secrets needed!)
        uses: azure/login@v2
        with:
          client-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}
          tenant-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
          subscription-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID }}

      - name: Login to Azure Container Registry
        run: az acr login --name myregistry

      - name: Build and push
        run: |
          docker build -t $REGISTRY/$IMAGE_NAME:${{ github.sha }} .
          docker push $REGISTRY/$IMAGE_NAME:${{ github.sha }}

      - name: Deploy to Container Apps
        run: |
          az containerapp update \
            --name api-app \
            --resource-group rg-production \
            --image $REGISTRY/$IMAGE_NAME:${{ github.sha }}
```

## Azure DevOps Pipeline (azure-pipelines.yml)
```yaml
# azure-pipelines.yml — use if your team is on Azure DevOps (not GitHub)
trigger:
  branches:
    include: [main]

pool:
  vmImage: ubuntu-latest

variables:
  dockerRegistryServiceConnection: 'ACR-Connection'
  imageRepository: 'api-app'
  containerRegistry: 'myregistry.azurecr.io'
  tag: '$(Build.BuildId)'

stages:
  - stage: Build
    jobs:
      - job: BuildAndPush
        steps:
          - task: NodeTool@0
            inputs: { versionSpec: '22.x' }

          - script: npm ci && npm run build && npm test
            displayName: 'Install, Build & Test'

          - task: Docker@2
            displayName: 'Build and push image'
            inputs:
              command: buildAndPush
              repository: $(imageRepository)
              dockerfile: Dockerfile
              containerRegistry: $(dockerRegistryServiceConnection)
              tags: |
                $(tag)
                latest

  - stage: Deploy
    dependsOn: Build
    condition: succeeded()
    jobs:
      - deployment: DeployToProduction
        environment: Production   # Requires manual approval gate
        strategy:
          runOnce:
            deploy:
              steps:
                - task: AzureContainerApps@1
                  inputs:
                    azureSubscription: 'Azure-Service-Connection'
                    containerAppName: 'api-app'
                    resourceGroup: 'rg-production'
                    acrName: 'myregistry'
                    imageToDeploy: $(containerRegistry)/$(imageRepository):$(tag)
```

## Azure Key Vault Integration
```typescript
// Never hardcode secrets — use Key Vault references
// In Container Apps, secrets are injected automatically via Key Vault reference

// For Node.js apps using the SDK:
import { SecretClient } from '@azure/keyvault-secrets';
import { DefaultAzureCredential } from '@azure/identity';

const credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); // Uses Managed Identity in Azure
const client = new SecretClient('https://my-kv.vault.azure.net', credential);

const secret = await client.getSecret('DATABASE-URL');
const databaseUrl = secret.value!;
```
